name: criminal_justice_cross_border_data_stonehaven_tru_loy
premise: A Criminal Justice court operations desk is in a fast-moving incident involving criminal justice cross border data stonehaven tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the press briefing. Every delay increases due process, safety, and legitimacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Jonah Berg
agents.identity: I am Jonah Berg, accountable for a defensible call in Criminal Justice when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Gideon Sato
agents.identity: I am Gideon Sato, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Criminal Justice.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the press briefing. Evidence is partial. Jonah Berg must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Jonah Berg do?
